misconceptions.title: Incorrect Boundary Line Type
misconceptions.description: Students graph inequalities with solid lines when they require dashed lines or vice versa. Without proper distinction between strict and inclusive inequalities, the solution set is wrong.
successIndicators: Draws dashed lines for strict inequalities
Draws solid lines for inclusive inequalities

misconceptions.title: Improper Slope Identification
misconceptions.description: Confusion arises when converting an inequality into slope-intercept form. Errors occur if coefficients are treated directly as slopes without isolating y.

misconceptions.title: Wrong Variable Substitution
misconceptions.description: In solving systems algebraically, incorrect variables are substituted into equations. This happens when students solve for one variable but substitute incorrectly for another.
successIndicators: Verifies both variables in final solution

misconceptions.title: Fraction Mismanagement
misconceptions.description: During equation solving, students either fail to clear fractions or do so improperly, leading to incorrect results when combining terms.
